title: Wall-Mounted Rifle Storage Rack
summary: A wall-mounted 1x8 shelving unit with internal dividers spacing each slot, sized to hold long guns upright and off the floor.
description:
This rack is a simple divided shelving box, sized and slotted for long guns rather than general storage — internal dividers create individual slots wide enough for a stock and barrel to rest upright without leaning into the next one over. Build the outer box first from the top, bottom, and sides, then add the internal dividers evenly spaced across the interior width.

The back panel closes the unit and gives the wall-mounting screws something continuous to bite into, rather than mounting through the thinner divider edges alone. **Mount into wall studs, not just drywall** — a loaded rack full of long guns is genuinely heavy, and drywall anchors alone are not a reliable long-term mount for this kind of weight.

This is explicitly a storage rack, not a locking security safe: it keeps guns organized, upright, and off the floor, but provides no locking mechanism. **If secure storage away from children or unauthorized access is a requirement, this rack does not meet that need on its own** — pair it with a locking cabinet, trigger locks, or a dedicated safe as your situation requires.

materials.name: Wood screws, 3", for wall mounting into studs
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the outer box
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom to the sides with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue to form the outer box frame.

steps.title: Add the dividers
steps.body: Space and attach the internal dividers evenly across the interior width, creating individual slots for each gun.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Cut the back panel from 1/4" plywood and nail to all edges with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Locate wall studs with a stud finder and mount the rack with 3" screws driven into the studs, checking level before final tightening.
materials: Wood screws, 3", for wall mounting into studs

unresolvedImages.alt: Wall-mounted rifle storage rack with divided slots
